macro_events.event_summary: Iran has reportedly closed the Strait of Hormuz again, firing on a vessel attempting to cross — threatening ~20% of global seaborne oil flows.
primary_drivers: Crude oil supply shock / risk premium spike
Tanker rerouting and shipping rate surge

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with large US/Guyana/Permian upstream production largely outside Hormuz — benefits directly from Brent price spike without proportionate asset risk. Every $10 Brent move ≈ ~$4B annual FCF sensitivity.
Similar to XOM — US-weighted upstream (Permian, DJ, TengizChevroil is non-Hormuz) captures crude price upside; no material tanker transit exposure through the Strait.
Pure-play Permian producer with zero Middle East exposure — highest torque to WTI/Brent spike among large-cap E&Ps due to unhedged production profile.
Frontline owns one of the largest VLCC fleets; Hormuz closure forces rerouting around Africa, collapsing tanker supply and sending VLCC day rates parabolic — direct revenue sensitivity.
Scorpio Tankers — product tanker rates spike as refined product trade reroutes; MR/LR tanker ton-miles surge when Gulf flows are disrupted.
Direct defense beneficiary — escalation increases demand for PAC-3, THAAD, and munitions replenishment for CENTCOM and Gulf allies. Headline defense bid.
Missile defense (SM-3, SM-6, Patriot) directly relevant to any kinetic Gulf response; Saudi/UAE replenishment orders likely if conflict widens.
Targa — US midstream/NGL export infrastructure becomes more valuable as buyers seek non-Hormuz LPG/ethane sourcing; LPG spreads widen.

losers.rationale: Delta has the highest jet fuel sensitivity among US airlines and minimal hedging — a sustained Brent spike of $15-20 directly compresses margins by hundreds of millions.
United also runs unhedged on jet fuel; international long-haul exposure adds rerouting/overflight cost risk if Gulf airspace becomes restricted.
Southwest is 100% domestic but fuel is ~25% of opex and hedges have rolled off; crude spike hits earnings directly with no international pricing offset.
Carnival — bunker fuel cost spike plus likely cancellation/rerouting of Middle East/Red Sea itineraries; consumer discretionary also sells off on oil-shock recession fear.
Dow Chemical — naphtha/ethane feedstock costs rise with crude; commodity chemicals can't fully pass through in a demand-weakening environment.
Consumer discretionary retailer hit by the classic oil-shock-as-consumer-tax dynamic; gasoline at the pump crowds out discretionary spend within 2-3 weeks.
Counterintuitive but: high-beta consumer discretionary with China exposure sells off in risk-off tapes; EV tailwind from high gas prices is slower than the multiple compression.

headlines: Iran says Strait of Hormuz is closed again as vessel attempting to cross comes under gunfire

macro_events.event_summary: Iran conflict and resulting $4 gasoline prices are suppressing US consumer discretionary spending, with concern that the window to revive consumer activity is narrowing.
primary_drivers: Higher gasoline prices reducing household discretionary income
Geopolitical risk premium in crude oil
Consumer confidence shock from war headlines

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with large upstream exposure; direct beneficiary of elevated Brent/WTI from Iran conflict risk premium. Every $10 sustained rise in crude flows meaningfully to EPS.
Same integrated oil thesis as XOM; US-listed pure-play on elevated crude. Upstream volumes leverage directly to realized prices.
High-beta US shale producer with the most torque to crude prices among large caps; Permian-concentrated, limited Middle East asset risk.
Prime defense contractor with direct exposure to missile defense (PAC-3, THAAD) and munitions replenishment demand from active Iran conflict.
Raytheon makes Patriot/SM-series interceptors actively consumed in Middle East operations; supplemental defense budget tailwind.
Consumer trade-down beneficiary: when gas at $4 squeezes middle-income budgets, share shifts to low-price grocery/essentials leader. Demonstrated pattern in prior fuel-price shocks.
Dollar General serves the rural/low-income consumer most hurt by gasoline prices; historically gains share during fuel-price stress despite own freight cost pressure.

losers.rationale: Carnival: discretionary travel + bunker fuel cost exposure + consumer pullback. Double hit from weaker demand and higher fuel opex.
Same cruise-line thesis as CCL with thinner balance sheet; highly discretionary consumer spend category first to be cut when gas bills rise.
Airline jet fuel is ~25-30% of opex and tracks crude; combined with weakening leisure travel demand from consumer stress, margins compress from both sides.
Most leveraged balance sheet among majors; highest sensitivity to simultaneous fuel cost rise and discretionary travel pullback.
Chipotle skews to middle-income consumer with elevated ticket price; traffic historically rolls over when gas >$4 forces trade-down to home/QSR value menus.
Restoration Hardware tied to big-ticket discretionary home furnishings — exactly the category consumers defer when macro confidence fades and gas bills bite.
Macy's mid-tier apparel demand historically the clearest casualty of gasoline-driven discretionary compression; category comp trends correlate inversely to pump prices.
Las Vegas Sands + broader gaming exposure to discretionary travel/leisure that gets cut first; also Asia exposure if conflict broadens risk-off.

headlines: Iran war, $4 gas, took all the fun out of the U.S. consumer economy. There's only so much time to bring it back

macro_events.event_summary: Trump's expected Fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh is signaling willingness to cut interest rates, raising expectations of a more dovish Fed regime.
primary_drivers: Lower discount rate boosts long-duration asset valuations
Lower funding costs for rate-sensitive borrowers (homebuilders, REITs, regional banks' deposit franchises)
Potential USD weakness from dovish pivot benefits commodity and multinational exporters

beneficiaries.rationale: D.R. Horton is the largest US homebuilder; mortgage rate cuts directly drive order volume and cancellation rates. Dovish Fed transition would reprice 30Y mortgage expectations lower.
Toll Brothers has high duration sensitivity via luxury buyer financing and jumbo mortgage spreads; benefits from both rate cuts and steeper curve narrative under Warsh.
High-multiple growth name whose valuation is dominated by out-year cash flows; explicit duration play on lower-for-longer rate path.

losers.rationale: Large-bank NII compresses as deposit betas lag asset repricing in a cutting cycle; JPM has guided to NII headwind from lower rates specifically.
Charles Schwab's cash sweep revenue is directly tied to short rates; every 25bp cut mechanically reduces net interest revenue on sweep balances.
Life insurers' investment portfolios reprice lower into a cutting cycle; spread business compresses and reinvestment yields fall.

headlines: Trump expects his Fed chair nominee to cut interest rates. Here’s how Kevin Warsh might try to do it.

macro_events.event_summary: Post-Iran war analysis piece arguing the US has emerged as the world's most secure energy power, implying diminished Middle East oil risk premium and elevated status of US domestic energy producers/exporters.
primary_drivers: Structural shift of global energy supply toward US producers / LNG exporters
Compression of Middle East geopolitical risk premium in crude
Increased strategic value of US energy export infrastructure

beneficiaries.rationale: Cheniere Energy is the largest US LNG exporter; a narrative of US energy dominance directly supports long-term offtake demand from European and Asian buyers diversifying away from Middle East/Russian supply.
Largest US natural gas producer by volume; pure-play domestic gas leverage to a 'US as secure energy supplier' thesis that pulls more gas into LNG export feedstock.
Permian production + Guyana + LNG (Golden Pass) makes Exxon the clearest integrated beneficiary of a shift in global energy gravity toward Western Hemisphere supply.
Diamondback is a pure-play Permian producer; post-war narrative of US as swing producer supports volume growth sentiment even if crude risk premium compresses.
Venture Global is a pure-play US LNG exporter (Calcasieu Pass, Plaquemines) directly leveraged to the 'US secure energy power' export thesis.

headlines: The real outcome of the Iran war: America is now the world’s most secure energy power

macro_events.event_summary: Iran claims the Strait of Hormuz is "under strict control," suggesting no imminent blockade, likely easing the oil risk premium as markets also await Tesla earnings.
primary_drivers: De-escalation of Hormuz chokepoint risk compressing crude geopolitical premium
Lower oil prices reducing input costs for fuel-intensive businesses

beneficiaries.rationale: Jet fuel is ~20-25% of operating costs; a compression of the Hormuz risk premium directly lowers Brent-linked jet fuel and expands near-term margins.
Same jet fuel cost sensitivity as DAL; international route exposure makes it a high-beta play on oil de-escalation.
Southwest historically runs lighter fuel hedges, so spot Brent declines flow through to earnings faster than peers.
Bunker fuel is a major cost line for cruise operators; lower crude on Hormuz de-escalation is a direct margin tailwind.
Fuel is a top-3 operating expense for air/ground freight; oil risk-off translates directly to opex relief.

losers.rationale: Integrated major with upstream leverage to Brent; compression of the geopolitical risk premium is a direct headwind to realized prices.
Permian-heavy pure-play E&P with high beta to WTI/Brent; unwind of Hormuz premium hits cash flow sensitivity directly.
Small/mid-cap E&P with highest beta to oil spot in the group; positioning bought Hormuz risk, de-escalation forces an unwind.
Permian pure-play; directly geared to spot oil price, loses the fear bid as Iran signals restraint.
Oilfield services revenues track upstream capex decisions which are anchored to crude futures; lower prices = soft capex tone.
Middle East de-escalation narrative reduces the defense/geopolitics bid that has supported primes; sentiment rotation out.

headlines: Dow Jones Futures: Iran Says Hormuz 'Under Strict Control'; Tesla Earnings Due
